hook: Good boundaries reduce confusion for both people.
body: When your limits are clearer, others can choose around them instead of guessing and testing.

hook: Hinting at preferences often creates more friction than just saying the preference directly.
body: Boundaries are part of honest communication, not a sign that the interaction became hostile.

body: Telling someone you prefer to plan ahead is more useful than hoping they will eventually infer it.

body: Replace one repeated hint with one direct sentence that says what works for you.
